mission.title: Install and Configure KubeRay
mission.description: KubeRay is an open-source Kubernetes operator designed to simplify the deployment and management of Ray applications on Kubernetes. It provides custom resource definitions for managing Ray clusters, jobs, and services, making it ideal for machine learning workloads. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in managing resources.

steps.title: Add the KubeRay Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeRay Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeRay using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kuberay https://ray-project.github.io/kuberay

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the KubeRay repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeRay CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeRay to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/raycluster.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayjob.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayservice.yaml

```

> Installs the RayCluster CRD.
> Installs the RayJob CRD.
> Installs the RayService CRD.

steps.title: Install KubeRay using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the KubeRay operator in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install kuberay kuberay/kuberay --namespace kuberay --create-namespace --version 1.5.1

```

> Installs KubeRay in the 'kuberay' namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeRay installation
steps.description:
This step checks whether the KubeRay operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

kubectl get crd

```

> Lists all pods in the 'kuberay' namespace to verify the installation.
> Verifies that the CRDs are installed.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeRay
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeRay and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all Ray clusters and jobs, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kuberay --namespace kuberay

kubectl delete namespace kuberay

```

> Uninstalls the KubeRay Helm release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeRay
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step upgrades KubeRay to the latest version and verifies the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay -o yaml > rayclusters-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kuberay kuberay/kuberay --namespace kuberay --version 1.5.1

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

```

> Backs up existing RayCluster configurations.
> Upgrades KubeRay to the specified version.
> Verifies that the KubeRay pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay -o yaml > rayclusters-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RayCluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Kubernetes cluster does not have sufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your cluster has enough CPU and memory resources allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'RayJob failed to start'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the RayCluster is not in a ready state.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the RayCluster with 'kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay' and ensure it is ready.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl command-line tool is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl by following the official installation guide for your operating system.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the KubeRay Helm release has not been installed.

**Fix:**
Run the installation commands to install KubeRay using Helm.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Ray features and improved stability of RayJob management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeRay operator running with the command 'kubectl get pods -n kuberay' showing the status as 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources and the necessary tools installed.
